# update-self-hosted-supabase-edge-function-env-vars
Description: Update environment variables for self-hosted Supabase Edge Functions and ensure they are picked up without a full stack restart.
Source: project
1. Update the necessary environment variables in your secrets management system or directly in the `.env` file for your self-hosted Supabase setup.
2. Ensure all required variables, including any service-specific keys and general Supabase access keys, are present and correctly configured.
3. Generate or update the `.env` file located in the volume mounted by your Supabase functions container. This file should contain all the environment variables needed by your edge functions.
4. Transfer this updated `.env` file to the appropriate location on your self-hosted server.
5. Access your server's command line.
6. Restart only the `functions` container using your container orchestration tool. For example, if using Docker Compose, execute `docker compose restart functions --no-deps`.
7. The edge functions will now pick up the new environment variables upon their next invocation. A full stack restart is not required, only the `functions` container needs to be restarted.
